content:
![](https://macsfvent.sgp1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/media/tumblr/tumblr_krmw14AgJt1qzwi7fo1_r1_500.gif)

Panic Chronicle / 2009-10-17

My attempt to solve the panic issue has failed. Even after clean installing of Snow Leopard with minimal set of 3rd-party software, the latest panic came when I wake up the machine this morning (that's 10 minutes ago). Could it be a hardware issue?

Found more info regarding `pmap.c` ...

`*	Remove a range of hardware page-table entries.`
`*	The entries given are the first (inclusive)`
`*	and last (exclusive) entries for the VM pages.`

(I don't know whether "unloaded kexts" has anything to do with this either.)

`unloaded kexts:`
`com.apple.driver.AppleHIDKeyboard	1.1.5f1`
